profile.description: NVIDIA Corporation stands as a prominent provider of advanced graphics, computational, and networking solutions, operating across the United States, Taiwan, China, and numerous international markets. Its Graphics division encompasses GeForce GPUs, central to PC gaming and personal computing experiences, along with the GeForce NOW cloud gaming service and its supporting infrastructure, as well as dedicated solutions for various gaming platforms. For professional visualization, it provides Quadro and NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstations, further offering vGPU software designed for cloud-centric visual and virtual computing, automotive platforms for in-vehicle infotainment, and the Omniverse software suite, facilitating 3D design and virtual world creation. The Compute & Networking segment is a cornerstone for AI, high-performance computing (HPC), and accelerated data center platforms. It integrates Mellanox networking and interconnect solutions, delivers automotive AI Cockpit technologies, fosters autonomous driving development through strategic agreements, and offers comprehensive autonomous vehicle solutions. This segment also manufactures cryptocurrency mining processors, supplies Jetson platforms for robotics and other embedded applications, and offers enterprise AI software, including NVIDIA AI Enterprise. These diverse offerings find widespread application across the gaming, professional visualization, data center, and automotive sectors. NVIDIA distributes its portfolio through a broad ecosystem, engaging original equipment and device manufacturers, system integrators, add-in board makers, retail channels, software vendors, internet and cloud service providers, automotive companies (both manufacturers and tier-1 suppliers), mapping firms, nascent technology ventures, and other industry stakeholders. A notable strategic partnership exists with Kroger Co. Founded in 1993, NVIDIA Corporation maintains its corporate headquarters in Santa Clara, California.

sourced_reports.title: NVIDIA Corp – Growth powered by market-leading, best-in-class GPUs
sourced_reports.provider: DBS Bank – DBS Group Research (US Equity Research)
sourced_reports.summary: DBS Group Research’s 25 May 2026 note reiterates a BUY on NVIDIA with a 12‑month target price of USD 250, arguing that long‑term structural AI demand, yearly accelerator upgrades (Blackwell to Rubin), and management’s USD 1 trillion cumulative AI‑chip revenue ambition through FY 2028 underpin continued hyper‑growth and premium valuation. The report emphasizes strong near‑ and medium‑term revenue visibility driven by hyperscaler and sovereign AI capex, while highlighting risks from increased scrutiny as the world’s largest chipmaker, intensifying competition, AI/DC growth normalization, tariffs, and broader macro or regulatory shocks.

sourced_reports.title: NVIDIA: A leader amid a transformational mega trend; Initiating Coverage with Outperform
sourced_reports.provider: Banco Safra – O Especialista (Global TMT Equity Research)
sourced_reports.summary: Safra’s initiation of coverage on 29 April 2026 assigns NVIDIA an Outperform rating and a YE 2026 target price of USD 300 (~40% upside), presenting the company as evolving from a cyclical semiconductor designer into a full‑stack AI infrastructure platform with a four‑layer moat (architecture, proprietary networking, privileged manufacturing access, and CUDA software) and increasingly “platform‑like” recurring upgrade economics. The thesis leans on an “AI factory” framework, robust projected revenue CAGR (~29.5% FY 2026–31), and massive cash‑flow generation with substantial capital returns, while calling out key risks around hyperscaler concentration, potential AI‑capex deceleration, ASIC and in‑house silicon competition, supply‑chain/geopolitical exposure, and power‑grid constraints.

sourced_reports.title: NVIDIA CORP (NVDA)
sourced_reports.provider: University of Iowa – Krause Fund (student‑managed fund, Tippie College of Business)
sourced_reports.summary: The 22 April 2026 Krause Fund report rates NVIDIA HOLD with a DCF‑anchored valuation range of USD 195–215, implying roughly flat to slightly negative near‑term total return from the then‑current price around USD 208 and concluding that upside is limited after a steep multi‑year run. It frames NVIDIA as a dominant AI‑accelerator and compute‑networking platform (~90%+ AI‑accelerator share) but models rapid growth in Compute & Networking decelerating from ~50% YoY in 2026E toward 10% by 2035E as competition (AMD, Intel and hyperscaler ASICs) builds, while stressing rising hyperscaler dependence, possible normalization of pricing power and margins (toward ~70% gross), and scenarios where AI demand or competitive pressure surprise positively or negatively versus base‑case assumptions.

sourced_reports.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) 股票深度研究报告—2026年4月
sourced_reports.provider: Transurf Research (independent Chinese equity analysis site)
sourced_reports.summary: This 9 April 2026 Chinese‑language deep‑dive views NVIDIA as the core infrastructure enabler of global AI, projecting FY 2026 revenue around USD 2.16 trillion (in local unit notation) with ~65% growth, very high gross margins (~71%) and free‑cash‑flow margins in the mid‑40s, and arguing that current valuation (mid‑30s FY 2026 P/E, low‑20s FY 2027E P/E) is justified by exceptional ROIC and capital‑light fabless economics. The report lays out a strongly positive long‑term thesis centered on CUDA’s ecosystem lock‑in, Blackwell and upcoming Vera Rubin architectures, tight upstream capacity (TSMC advanced nodes, CoWoS, HBM), and hyperscaler AI‑capex trends, while also detailing bear‑case risks around customer concentration, potential AI “ROI reckoning,” competition from AMD and custom accelerators (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, etc.), export controls, and normalization of today’s peak‑level margins and multiples.

sourced_reports.title: Nvidia: The Core Enabler of the Global AI Economy
sourced_reports.provider: University of Iowa – Krause Fund (Applied Securities Management / student‑managed fund)
sourced_reports.summary: The Fall 2025 Krause Fund report, dated 16 November 2025, assigns NVIDIA a BUY rating with an intrinsic value estimate of about USD 198.22 per share versus a then‑current price near USD 182.18, characterizing the company as the “core enabler of the global AI economy” through its leading GPUs, data‑center platforms, and CUDA‑centric software stack. It develops a multi‑method valuation (DCF, economic profit, DDM and comps), emphasizing ultra‑high margins and ROIC, explosive Compute & Networking growth (projected 80%+ in the near term), and secular AI demand, while explicitly flagging risks such as high valuation and potential “AI bubble” dynamics, increased competition in accelerated computing, supply‑chain and export‑control exposure, and macro variables like interest rates, GDP growth, inflation and consumer‑spending trends.

sourced_reports.title: Nvidia Corp – US EQUITY RESEARCH
sourced_reports.provider: DBS Bank – DBS Group Research (US Equity Research)
sourced_reports.summary: In its 1 September 2025 US Equity Research report, DBS maintains a BUY rating on NVIDIA with a higher target price of USD 220 (from USD 180), arguing that sustained AI/data‑center capex, the ongoing Blackwell ramp and potential H20 shipments into China support a path to more than USD 200 billion of FY 2026 sales (roughly +56% YoY) and justify premium P/E and EV/EBITDA multiples. The note underscores NVIDIA’s market‑leading AI chips, structurally above‑peer margins, strong cash generation and net‑cash balance sheet as key strengths, but also highlights risk factors including uncertainty over China revenues (export‑control‑dependent H20 upside), rising competition in AI/ML silicon, geopolitical tensions and tariffs, and the elevated expectations that come with being one of the world’s largest and most richly valued stocks.
